1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# scripts/benchmark/benchmark — operator-facing benchmark driver.
3	#
4	# Replaces `fiz-bench sweep` / `fiz-bench matrix` execution. Pure shell:
5	# yq + jq + sha256sum + docker. No Go bridge, no Python.
6	#
7	# Bead fizeau-fe87a3ac: skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode +
8	# preflight rebuild gate. The cell execution loop (resume, retry, signal
9	# handling, concurrency, budget) lands in a follow-up bead.
10	#
11	# Operator contract:
12	#   ./benchmark profiles
13	#   ./benchmark bench-sets
14	#   ./benchmark harness-adapters
15	#   ./benchmark task-executors
16	#   ./benchmark validate                            # placeholder; see PR C
17	#   ./benchmark preflight                           # rebuild harbor-runner if sha drifted
18	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] --plan
19	#   ./benchmark --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...]   # run path; PR A4
20	#
21	# Rules:
22	#   1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls/builds, no
23	#      benchmark-results/ creation. Prints one matrix line per cell.
24	#   2. --profile and --bench-set are both required for --plan and run.
25	#   3. preflight is the only command that touches Docker.

82	# acquire_group_lock acquires an flock for a concurrency group, blocking if needed.
83	# Returns 0 on success, prints the lock fd to stdout.
84	acquire_group_lock() {
85	  local group="$1"
86	  local lock_path
87	  lock_path="$(in_flight_lock_path "${group}")"
88	  mkdir -p "$(dirname "${lock_path}")"
89	  local lock_fd
90	  lock_fd=200
91	  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>'${lock_path}'"
92	  flock "${lock_fd}" || die "failed to acquire lock for concurrency-group=${group}"
93	  printf '%d\n' "${lock_fd}"
94	}

96	# release_group_lock releases an flock acquired by acquire_group_lock.
97	release_group_lock() {
98	  local lock_fd="$1"
99	  eval "exec ${lock_fd}>&-" || true
100	}

152	# register_inflight registers a cell as in-flight in the state file.
153	# Reads current entries, adds this cell, removes dead PIDs, writes back.
154	register_inflight() {
155	  local cell_id="$1" cell_dir="$2"
156	  local state_dir
157	  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
158	  mkdir -p "${state_dir}"
159	  local json_path
160	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
161	
162	  local current_json="{\"cells\":[]}"
163	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] && current_json="$(cat "${json_path}")" || true
164	
165	  # Prune dead PIDs by filtering in bash
166	  local live_cells=()
167	  local cell_json pid
168	  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
169	    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
170	    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
171	    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
172	      live_cells+=("${cell_json}")
173	    fi
174	  done < <(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' <<<"${current_json}")
175	
176	  # Rebuild with live cells
177	  current_json="$(printf '%s\n' "${live_cells[@]}" | jq -s '{cells:.}')"
178	
179	  # Add this cell
180	  current_json="$(jq \
181	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
182	    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
183	    --argjson pid "${BASHPID}" \
184	    '.cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, cell_dir:$cell_dir, pid:$pid}]' \
185	    <<<"${current_json}")"
186	
187	  printf '%s\n' "${current_json}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
188	  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
189	}

196	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
197	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0
198	
199	  local updated
200	  updated="$(jq \
201	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
202	    '.cells |= map(select(.cell_id != $cell_id))' \
203	    "${json_path}")"

209	# interrupt_inflight_cells is called during signal handling to mark in-flight
210	# cells as interrupted and clean them up from the JSON file.
211	interrupt_inflight_cells() {
212	  local json_path
213	  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
214	  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0
215	
216	  local cells
217	  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}")" || return 0
218	
219	  local cell_id cell_dir pid
220	  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
221	    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
222	    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${cell_json}")"
223	    cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${cell_json}")"
224	    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
225	
226	    # Try to kill the cell's process group
227	    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
228	      kill -TERM -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || kill -TERM "${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
229	    fi
230	
231	    # Try to docker stop any harbor-runner containers in this cell
232	    if [[ -d "${cell_dir}" ]]; then
233	      # The container ID is typically embedded in result.json or we can search by cell_dir
234	      # For now, we attempt a generic docker stop on containers that might be for this cell
235	      docker ps -q --filter "label=fizeau-cell=${cell_id}" 2>/dev/null \
236	        | xargs -r docker stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
237	    fi
238	  done <<<"${cells}"
239	}

308	# list_adapter_dir prints one "<name>\t<summary>" line per executable file.
309	# Files without a `# SUMMARY:` line on line 2 print "(no summary)" so the
310	# omission is visible. CONTRACT.md and other non-executables are skipped.
311	list_adapter_dir() {
312	  local dir="$1"
313	  [[ -d "${dir}" ]] || { warn "directory missing: ${dir}"; return 0; }
314	  local f name summary
315	  local rows=()
316	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
317	  for f in "${dir}"/*; do
318	    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
319	    name="$(basename "${f}")"
320	    summary="$(sed -n '2{/^# SUMMARY:[[:space:]]*/{s///;p;};q;}' "${f}")"
321	    [[ -n "${summary}" ]] || summary="(no summary)"
322	    rows+=("$(printf '%s\t%s' "${name}" "${summary}")")
323	  done
324	  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
325	  if [[ ${#rows[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
326	    warn "no adapters found in ${dir}"
327	    return 0
328	  fi
329	  printf '%s\n' "${rows[@]}" | LC_ALL=C sort -u
330	}

361	# resolve_tasks emits one task id per line for a bench-set.
362	# Handles three shapes:
363	#   tasks: [id, id, ...]         inline list
364	#   tasks: all                   defer to harness dataset; emit "*all*"
365	#   tasks_from: <path>           load tasks[].id from a sibling YAML
366	# tasks_from and inline tasks merge in deterministic order with duplicates
367	# removed on first occurrence.
368	resolve_tasks() {
369	  local bs_file="$1"
370	  local bs_json tasks_kind tasks_from
371	  bs_json="$(bench_set_json "${bs_file}")"
372	
373	  tasks_kind="$(jq -r '
374	    if (.tasks // null) == null then "none"
375	    elif (.tasks | type) == "array" then "list"
376	    elif (.tasks | type) == "string" then ("string:" + .tasks)
377	    else "other"
378	    end' <<<"${bs_json}")"
379	
380	  tasks_from="$(jq -r '.tasks_from // ""' <<<"${bs_json}")"
381	
382	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "string:all" ]]; then
383	    [[ -z "${tasks_from}" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_file}: tasks: all cannot be combined with tasks_from"
384	    # Defer to the harness dataset. The plan path emits a sentinel so
385	    # operators see the contract; the run path (PR A4) expands at execution.
386	    printf '*all*\n'
387	    return 0
388	  fi
389	
390	  local -A seen_tasks=()
391	  local emitted=0
392	  local task resolved
393	
394	  emit_task() {
395	    local task="$1"
396	    [[ -n "${task}" ]] || return 0
397	    if [[ -z "${seen_tasks["${task}"]+x}" ]]; then
398	      seen_tasks["${task}"]=1
399	      printf '%s\n' "${task}"
400	      emitted=1
401	    fi
402	  }
403	
404	  if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" ]]; then
405	    resolved="$(cd "$(dirname "${bs_file}")" && cd "$(dirname "${tasks_from}")" && pwd)/$(basename "${tasks_from}")"
406	    [[ -f "${resolved}" ]] || die "tasks_from path not found: ${tasks_from} (resolved to ${resolved})"
407	    while IFS= read -r task; do
408	      emit_task "${task}"
409	    done < <(
410	      yq -o=json '.' "${resolved}" \
411	        | jq -r '
412	            .tasks[]?
413	            | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)
414	          '
415	    )
416	  fi
417	
418	  if [[ "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
419	    while IFS= read -r task; do
420	      emit_task "${task}"
421	    done < <(jq -r '
422	      .tasks[] | (if type == "object" then (.id // empty) else . end)
423	    ' <<<"${bs_json}")
424	  elif [[ "${tasks_kind}" != "none" ]]; then
425	    die "bench-set ${bs_file}: unsupported tasks shape"
426	  fi
427	
428	  if [[ ${emitted} -eq 0 ]]; then
429	    if [[ -n "${tasks_from}" || "${tasks_kind}" == "list" ]]; then
430	      die "bench-set ${bs_file}: resolved no tasks"
431	    fi
432	    die "bench-set ${bs_file}: no .tasks list, .tasks: all, or .tasks_from set"
433	  fi
434	}

595	# validate_harness_adapters checks adapter install/command JSON contracts for
596	# every executable in scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/.
597	validate_harness_adapters() {
598	  local tmpdir="$1" synthetic_profile="$2"
599	  local artifact="${tmpdir}/agent-artifact"
600	  : >"${artifact}"
601	
602	  local f name install_out command_out install_err command_err install_status command_status
603	  local files=()
604	  shopt -s nullglob dotglob
605	  for f in "${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"/*; do
606	    [[ -f "${f}" && -x "${f}" ]] || continue
607	    files+=("${f}")
608	  done
609	  shopt -u nullglob dotglob
610	  [[ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]] || die "validate: no harness adapters found in ${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}"
611	
612	  for f in "${files[@]}"; do
613	    name="$(basename "${f}")"
614	    install_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.install.err"
615	    command_err="${tmpdir}/${name}.command.err"
616	
617	    set +e
618	    install_out="$("${f}" install "${artifact}" 2>"${install_err}")"
619	    install_status=$?
620	    set -e
621	    if [[ ${install_status} -ne 0 ]]; then
622	      die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} install failed: $(<"${install_err}")"
623	    fi
624	    jq -e '
625	      type == "object"
626	      and (.install_command | type == "string" and length > 0)
627	      and (.artifact_source | type == "string" and length > 0)
628	      and (.binary_path | type == "string")
629	      and (has("harbor_plugin"))
630	    ' <<<"${install_out}" >/dev/null \
631	      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} install output is malformed"
632	
633	    set +e
634	    command_out="$(printf '%s' "${synthetic_profile}" | "${f}" command 2>"${command_err}")"
635	    command_status=$?
636	    set -e
637	    if [[ ${command_status} -ne 0 ]]; then
638	      die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command failed: $(<"${command_err}")"
639	    fi
640	    jq -e '
641	      type == "object"
642	      and (.command | type == "array" and length > 0)
643	      and all(.command[]?; type == "string")
644	      and (.env | type == "object")
645	      and all((.env // {}) | to_entries[]?; (.value | type == "string"))
646	      and (.secret_env_keys | type == "array")
647	      and ((.secret_env_keys | unique | length) == (.secret_env_keys | length))
648	      and (.env as $env | all(.secret_env_keys[]?; $env[.] != null))
649	    ' <<<"${command_out}" >/dev/null \
650	      || die "validate: harness-adapter ${name} command output is malformed"
651	  done

696	# print_plan emits one line per (profile, task, rep) cell. Pure: no writes.
697	print_plan() {
698	  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" reps_override="${3:-}"
699	  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
700	  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"
701	
702	  local profile_id bs_id bs_file framework dataset reps task_executor tasks
703	  local rep i n
704	
705	  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
706	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
707	    resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}" >/dev/null  # existence check
708	    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
709	      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
710	      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
711	      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
712	      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
713	      if [[ -n "${reps_override}" ]]; then
714	        reps="${reps_override}"
715	      else

717	      fi
718	      task_executor="$(resolve_bench_set_task_executor "${bs_file}")"
719	      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
720	      i=0
721	      while IFS= read -r task; do
722	        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
723	        i=$((i + 1))
724	        for ((rep = 1; rep <= reps; rep++)); do
725	          n=$((rep))
726	          printf 'profile=%s\tbench_set=%s\tframework=%s\tdataset=%s\ttask=%s\trep=%d/%d\ttask_executor=%s\n' \
727	            "${profile_id}" "${bs_id}" "${framework}" "${dataset}" \
728	            "${task}" "${n}" "${reps}" "${task_executor}"
729	        done
730	      done <<<"${tasks}"
731	      [[ "${i}" -gt 0 ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: no tasks resolved"
732	    done

737	# preflight: rebuild harbor-runner if image-content-sha drifted

739	
740	# compute_harbor_source_sha mirrors build-harbor-runner.sh's algorithm: a
741	# sha256 over harbor_adapters/* + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.
742	# Kept in sync by re-computing the same inputs; the two scripts MUST agree.
743	compute_harbor_source_sha() {
744	  local adapters_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_adapters"
745	  local agent_file="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_agent.py"
746	  local dockerfile="${SCRIPT_DIR}/Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
747	  [[ -d "${adapters_dir}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_adapters/ missing (${adapters_dir})"
748	  [[ -f "${agent_file}" ]] || die "preflight: harbor_agent.py missing (${agent_file})"
749	  [[ -f "${dockerfile}" ]] || die "preflight: Dockerfile.harbor-runner missing (${dockerfile})"
750	  {
751	    LC_ALL=C find "${adapters_dir}" -type f \
752	      | LC_ALL=C sort \
753	      | while IFS= read -r f; do
754	          printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${f}" | awk '{print $1}')" "${f#"${SCRIPT_DIR}/"}"
755	        done
756	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${agent_file}" | awk '{print $1}')" "harbor_agent.py"
757	    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${dockerfile}" | awk '{print $1}')" "Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
758	  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
759	}

761	# cached_harbor_image_sha prints the image-content-sha label of the cached
762	# image, or empty if the image is absent / the label is missing.
763	cached_harbor_image_sha() {

765	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 || { printf ''; return 0; }
766	  docker image inspect --format '{{ index .Config.Labels "image-content-sha" }}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || printf ''

769	cmd_preflight() {
770	  require_tool sha256sum
771	
772	  local failed=0
773	  local source_sha cached_sha
774	  local state_dir host_state_dir lock_dir probe
775	  local build_output build_rc
776	
777	  printf 'preflight checklist:\n'
778	
779	  check_tool() {
780	    local tool="$1"
781	    if command -v "${tool}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
782	      printf '  [x] %s (%s)\n' "${tool}" "$(command -v "${tool}")"
783	      return 0
784	    fi
785	    printf '  [ ] %s (missing)\n' "${tool}"
786	    failed=1
787	    return 1
788	  }
789	
790	  check_writable_dir() {
791	    local label="$1" dir="$2" tmp
792	    if mkdir -p "${dir}" 2>/dev/null; then
793	      tmp="${dir}/.preflight.$$"
794	      if : >"${tmp}" 2>/dev/null; then
795	        rm -f "${tmp}"
796	        printf '  [x] %s (%s)\n' "${label}" "${dir}"
797	        return 0
798	      fi
799	    fi
800	    printf '  [ ] %s (%s not writable)\n' "${label}" "${dir}"
801	    failed=1
802	    return 1
803	  }
804	
805	  check_tool yq
806	  check_tool jq
807	  check_tool docker
808	  check_tool setsid
809	  check_tool flock
810	
811	  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
812	  lock_dir="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/locks"
813	  check_writable_dir "benchmark state dir" "${state_dir}"
814	  check_writable_dir "benchmark lock dir" "${lock_dir}"
815	
816	  source_sha="$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
817	  cached_sha="$(cached_harbor_image_sha "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}")"
818	  printf '  [x] harbor source fingerprint (%s)\n' "${source_sha}"
819	  printf '  [x] cached harbor image fingerprint (%s)\n' "${cached_sha:-<none>}"
820	
821	  if [[ -n "${cached_sha}" && "${cached_sha}" == "${source_sha}" ]]; then
822	    printf '  [x] harbor-runner image up to date (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
823	  else
824	    if [[ ! -x "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" ]]; then
825	      printf '  [ ] harbor-runner build script executable (%s missing or not executable)\n' "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
826	      failed=1
827	    else
828	      printf '  [*] building harbor-runner image via %s\n' "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}"
829	      set +e
830	      build_output="$(IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" 2>&1)"
831	      build_rc=$?
832	      set -e
833	      printf '%s\n' "${build_output}"
834	      if [[ ${build_rc} -eq 0 ]]; then
835	        printf '  [x] harbor-runner image buildable (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
836	      else
837	        printf '  [ ] harbor-runner image buildable (%s)\n' "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
838	        failed=1
839	      fi
840	    fi
841	  fi
842	
843	  if [[ ${failed} -ne 0 ]]; then
844	    printf 'preflight: one or more checks failed\n' >&2
845	    return 1
846	  fi
847	
848	  printf 'preflight: all checks passed\n'
849	}

855	# Terminal final_status values that satisfy resume coverage. Non-terminal
856	# statuses (running, transient_exhausted, missing_result) do not count as
857	# coverage; --retry-invalid surfaces invalid_class != "" or orphan state.
858	is_terminal_status() {
859	  case "$1" in
860	    completed|pass|fail|timeout|invalid|completed_no_grade) return 0 ;;
861	    *) return 1 ;;
862	  esac
863	}

865	# is_transient_error_class checks if an error_class from result.json is
866	# retryable.
867	is_transient_error_class() {
868	  case "$1" in
869	    connection_refused|http_5xx|eof_parse|provider_timeout|dial_timeout) return 0 ;;
870	    *) return 1 ;;
871	  esac
872	}

874	# Map the profile's `harness:` field to a harness-adapter file name.

876	resolve_adapter_for_harness() {
877	  case "${1:-}" in
878	    ""|none) printf 'fiz\n' ;;
879	    anthropic) printf 'claude\n' ;;
880	    *)       printf '%s\n' "$1" ;;

884	# Mint a fresh cell-id: UTC timestamp + 4 random hex chars.
885	new_cell_id() {
886	  local ts rand
887	  ts="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
888	  rand="$(LC_ALL=C tr -dc 'a-f0-9' </dev/urandom 2>/dev/null | head -c 4 || true)"
889	  if [[ -z "${rand}" || ${#rand} -lt 4 ]]; then
890	    rand="$(printf '%04x' $((RANDOM & 0xFFFF)))"
891	  fi
892	  printf '%s-%s\n' "${ts}" "${rand}"

895	# task_executor_version: sha256 of the harbor task-executor script. The
896	# task-executor is shell, so the script bytes are the version of record.
897	task_executor_version() {
898	  local f="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"

906	# harbor_runner_image_digest: docker image Id for fizeau-harbor-runner. When
907	# docker is absent (CI / dry-run), fall back to the source-content sha so the
908	# sweep manifest is still well-defined.
909	harbor_runner_image_digest() {
910	  # Test/operator override wins so suites can pin the recorded digest.
911	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
912	    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE}"
913	    return 0
914	  fi
915	  local tag="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
916	  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
917	    local d
918	    d="$(docker image inspect --format '{{.Id}}' "${tag}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
919	    if [[ -n "${d}" ]]; then
920	      printf '%s\n' "${d}"
921	      return 0
922	    fi
923	  fi
924	  # No docker (or image absent). Derive from the source-content sha used by
925	  # preflight.
926	  if compute_harbor_source_sha >/dev/null 2>&1; then
927	    printf 'src:%s\n' "$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
928	  else
929	    printf 'unknown\n'
930	  fi
931	}

949	# compose_env_redacted prints command-spec env with secret_env_keys replaced
950	# by a redaction sentinel. Order-insensitive; entries missing from env are
951	# ignored.
952	compose_env_redacted() {
953	  local env_json="$1" secret_keys_json="$2"
954	  jq -c --argjson secrets "${secret_keys_json}" '
955	    . as $env
956	    | reduce ($secrets[]? | tostring) as $k
957	        ($env; if has($k) then .[$k] = "***REDACTED***" else . end)
958	  ' <<<"${env_json}"

961	# is_transient_stderr greps the executor's stderr for known transient error
962	# signatures: connection refused/reset, HTTP 5xx, unexpected/EOF parse.
963	is_transient_stderr() {
964	  local f="$1"
965	  [[ -s "${f}" ]] || return 1
966	  LC_ALL=C grep -qE 'connection refused|connection reset|HTTP 5[0-9][0-9]|status[ =:]?5[0-9][0-9]|status_code=5[0-9][0-9]|unexpected EOF|EOF parse|EOF while|temporarily unavailable' "${f}"
967	}

969	# Invoke the task-executor with bounded exponential backoff on transient
970	# errors. Returns 0 on success; non-zero on terminal failure after retries.
971	# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.
972	run_executor_with_retry() {
973	  local task_spec="$1" cell_dir="$2" executor="$3"
974	  local stderr_file
975	  stderr_file="$(mktemp)"
976	  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
977	  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
978	  local attempt=1
979	  local status=0
980	  local retry_log="${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt"
981	  : >"${retry_log}"
982	  while :; do
983	    : >"${stderr_file}"
984	    set +e
985	    printf '%s' "${task_spec}" | "${executor}" 2>"${stderr_file}" >/dev/null
986	    status=$?
987	    set -e
988	    printf 'attempt=%d status=%d\n' "${attempt}" "${status}" >>"${retry_log}"
989	    if [[ ${status} -eq 0 ]]; then
990	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
991	      return 0
992	    fi
993	    cat "${stderr_file}" >>"${retry_log}"
994	    if (( attempt > max_attempts )) || ! is_transient_stderr "${stderr_file}"; then
995	      cat "${stderr_file}" >&2
996	      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
997	      return "${status}"
998	    fi
999	    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}); sleeping ${backoff}s"
1000	    sleep "${backoff}"
1001	    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
1002	    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
1003	  done
1004	}

1020	# Count existing terminal cells matching (profile_id, task) under <out>; also
1021	# emit lists of invalid cells and orphan cells (cells with cell-state.json
1022	# and no terminal report.json). Lines are written to the named output paths.
1023	inspect_existing_cells() {
1024	  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4"
1025	  local terminal_out="$5" invalid_out="$6" orphan_out="$7"
1026	  : >"${terminal_out}"
1027	  : >"${invalid_out}"
1028	  : >"${orphan_out}"
1029	  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
1030	  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || return 0
1031	  local cell
1032	  shopt -s nullglob
1033	  for cell in "${task_dir}"/*/; do
1034	    local cdir="${cell%/}"
1035	    local report="${cdir}/report.json"
1036	    local state="${cdir}/cell-state.json"
1037	    if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
1038	      local pid status inv
1039	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1040	      [[ "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1041	      status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1042	      inv="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1043	      if [[ -n "${inv}" ]]; then
1044	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${invalid_out}"
1045	        continue
1046	      fi
1047	      if is_terminal_status "${status}"; then
1048	        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${terminal_out}"
1049	      fi
1050	      continue
1051	    fi
1052	    if [[ -f "${state}" ]]; then
1053	      local pid
1054	      pid="$(jq -r '.profile // ""' "${state}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1055	      [[ -z "${pid}" || "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1056	      printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${orphan_out}"
1057	    fi
1058	  done
1059	  shopt -u nullglob
1060	}

1062	# compute_cell_cost prints the cost_usd_at_run_time for a single cell. Uses
1063	# profile.pricing × token streams pulled from the cell's result.json. Token
1064	# field names follow the canonical report-level naming
1065	# (input_tokens / output_tokens / cached_input_tokens) and are also probed
1066	# under the harbor totals naming (total_prompt_tokens / total_completion_tokens
1067	# / total_cached_input_tokens) so harness-native token reporters land in the
1068	# same accumulator.
1069	#
1070	# When profile.pricing is all zero (or unset) and the executor wrote an
1071	# explicit cost_usd / total_cost_usd in result.json, the explicit value is
1072	# honored — that lets harnesses that observe API-reported cost directly
1073	# participate in budget enforcement.
1074	compute_cell_cost() {
1075	  local result_json="${1:-}" profile_json="${2:-}"
1076	  [[ -n "${result_json}" ]] || result_json='{}'
1077	  [[ -n "${profile_json}" ]] || profile_json='{}'
1078	  jq -nr \
1079	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
1080	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1081	    '
1082	      def num(v): if (v == null) then 0 else (v | tonumber? // 0) end;
1083	      ($profile.pricing // {}) as $p
1084	      | num($p.input_usd_per_mtok)         as $pi
1085	      | num($p.output_usd_per_mtok)        as $po
1086	      | num($p.cached_input_usd_per_mtok)  as $pc
1087	      | num($result.input_tokens
1088	            // $result.total_prompt_tokens
1089	            // $result.n_input_tokens
1090	            // 0)                          as $ti
1091	      | num($result.output_tokens
1092	            // $result.total_completion_tokens
1093	            // $result.n_output_tokens
1094	            // 0)                          as $to
1095	      | num($result.cached_input_tokens
1096	            // $result.total_cached_input_tokens
1097	            // $result.n_cached_input_tokens
1098	            // 0)                          as $tc
1099	      | num($result.cost_usd // $result.total_cost_usd // 0) as $reported
1100	      | (($ti * $pi) + ($to * $po) + ($tc * $pc)) / 1000000  as $priced
1101	      | (if $priced > 0 then $priced
1102	         elif ($pi + $po + $pc) == 0 then $reported
1103	         else 0
1104	         end)
1105	      | tostring
1106	    '
1107	}

1109	# budget_path prints the canonical budget.json path under <out>.
1110	budget_path() {
1111	  printf '%s\n' "$1/budget.json"
1112	}
1113	
1114	# init_budget writes <out>/budget.json with the configured cap and an empty
1115	# accumulator. Idempotent: preserves existing cells/total when the cap
1116	# matches (resume case).
1117	init_budget() {
1118	  local out="$1" cap="$2"
1119	  local bp
1120	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1121	  if [[ -f "${bp}" ]]; then
1122	    local prev_cap
1123	    prev_cap="$(jq -r '.max_cost_usd // ""' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
1124	    if [[ "${prev_cap}" == "${cap}" ]]; then
1125	      return 0
1126	    fi
1127	  fi
1128	  jq -n \
1129	    --argjson cap "${cap}" \
1130	    '{max_cost_usd:$cap, total_cost_usd:0, halted:false, cells:[]}' \
1131	    >"${bp}.tmp"
1132	  mv "${bp}.tmp" "${bp}"
1133	}

1135	# append_budget_cell folds one closed cell's cost into budget.json and prints
1136	# the post-update total_cost_usd. Halted flag flips true when the running
1137	# total meets or exceeds the cap.
1138	append_budget_cell() {
1139	  local out="$1" cell_id="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4" cost="$5"
1140	  local bp tmp
1141	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1142	  tmp="$(mktemp)"
1143	  jq \
1144	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1145	    --arg task "${task}" \
1146	    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1147	    --argjson cost "${cost}" \
1148	    '
1149	      .cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, cost_usd:$cost}]
1150	      | .total_cost_usd = ((.total_cost_usd // 0) + $cost)
1151	      | .halted = ((.total_cost_usd >= (.max_cost_usd // 0))
1152	                   and ((.max_cost_usd // 0) > 0))
1153	    ' "${bp}" >"${tmp}"
1154	  mv "${tmp}" "${bp}"
1155	  jq -r '.total_cost_usd' "${bp}"
1156	}

1158	# budget_is_halted exits 0 when the budget cap has been hit (halted=true in
1159	# budget.json). Used by run_sweep to gate cells before invoking the executor.
1160	budget_is_halted() {
1161	  local bp
1162	  bp="$(budget_path "$1")"
1163	  [[ -f "${bp}" ]] || return 1
1164	  local halted
1165	  halted="$(jq -r '.halted // false' "${bp}")"
1166	  [[ "${halted}" == "true" ]]
1167	}

1169	# write_budget_halted_cell writes a placeholder cell that did not execute
1170	# because the budget cap had already been hit. final_status=budget_halted,
1171	# process_outcome=setup_failed, plus a 'note' that quotes the cap.
1172	write_budget_halted_cell() {
1173	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1174	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1175	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" cap="${10}"
1176	  local te_ver="${11}" hd="${12}"
1177	
1178	  local cell_id cell_dir
1179	  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
1180	  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
1181	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}/session"
1182	  : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"
1183	  : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"
1184	
1185	  local profile_json bp total
1186	  profile_json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
1187	  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
1188	  total="$(jq -r '.total_cost_usd // 0' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || printf '0')"
1189	
1190	  local now rep_str note
1191	  now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1192	  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"
1193	  note="$(printf 'halted before cell: --max-cost-usd=%s reached (cumulative cost_usd_at_run_time=%s)' \

1196	  jq -n \
1197	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1198	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
1199	    --arg framework "${framework}" \
1200	    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
1201	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1202	    --arg now "${now}" \
1203	    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
1204	    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1205	    --arg hd "${hd}" \
1206	    --arg note "${note}" \
1207	    --argjson cost 0 \
1208	    '{
1209	       task_id:$task_id,
1210	       framework:$framework,
1211	       dataset:$dataset,
1212	       cell_id:$cell_id,
1213	       started_at:$now,
1214	       finished_at:$now,
1215	       rep:$rep,
1216	       profile:$profile,
1217	       command:[],
1218	       env_redacted:{},
1219	       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
1220	       result:{},
1221	       final_status:"budget_halted",
1222	       invalid_class:"",
1223	       process_outcome:"setup_failed",
1224	       note:$note,
1225	       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
1226	       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
1227	       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
1228	     }' >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
1229	  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"

1233	# Run one cell start-to-finish: cell-state sentinel, harness-adapter command,
1234	# task-spec composition, task-executor invocation with retry, report.json
1235	# composition, sentinel cleanup, optional back-write of superseded_by into
1236	# the prior (attempt_of) cell. Registers as in-flight at start, unregisters at end.
1237	run_one_cell() {
1238	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1239	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1240	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" harness_adapter="${10}"
1241	  local attempt_of="${11}"
1242	  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"
1243	  local interrupted_flag="${14:-}"
1244	
1245	  local cell_id cell_dir
1246	  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"
1247	  cell_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}/${cell_id}"
1248	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}"
1249	
1250	  # Handler for SIGTERM during cell execution
1251	  write_interrupted_report() {
1252	    local now
1253	    now="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1254	    jq -n \
1255	      --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1256	      --arg task "${task}" \
1257	      --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1258	      --arg now "${now}" \
1259	      --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1260	      --arg hd "${hd}" \
1261	      '{
1262	         cell_id:$cell_id,
1263	         task_id:$task,
1264	         framework:"terminal-bench",
1265	         dataset:"terminal-bench-2-1",
1266	         started_at:$now,
1267	         finished_at:$now,
1268	         rep:"1/1",
1269	         final_status:"interrupted",
1270	         invalid_class:"",
1271	         process_outcome:"killed",
1272	         cost_usd_at_run_time:0,
1273	         task_executor_version:$te_ver,
1274	         harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
1275	       }' >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
1276	    mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
1277	  }
1278	
1279	  # Register as in-flight so signal handlers can interrupt us
1280	  register_inflight "${cell_id}" "${cell_dir}"
1281	  trap 'write_interrupted_report; unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM; exit 143' TERM
1282	  trap 'unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"; trap - RETURN TERM' RETURN
1283	
1284	  # Check if global interrupt flag is set before starting
1285	  if [[ -n "${interrupted_flag}" && -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]]; then
1286	    warn "cell ${cell_id}: halting due to signal interrupt"
1287	    # Write interrupted report immediately
1288	    write_interrupted_report
1289	    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1290	    return 0
1291	  fi
1292	
1293	  local started_at
1294	  started_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1295	  jq -n \
1296	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1297	    --arg task "${task}" \
1298	    --arg profile "${profile_id}" \
1299	    --arg started "${started_at}" \
1300	    '{cell_id:$cell_id, task:$task, profile:$profile, started_at:$started, status:"running"}' \
1301	    >"${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
1302	
1303	  local profile_json
1304	  profile_json="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
1305	
1306	  local adapter_path="${HARNESS_ADAPTERS_DIR}/${harness_adapter}"
1307	  [[ -x "${adapter_path}" ]] || die "harness-adapter missing/not executable: ${adapter_path}"
1308	
1309	  local command_spec install_spec harbor_plugin
1310	  install_spec="$("${adapter_path}" install "/installed-agent/${harness_adapter}")" \
1311	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} install failed"
1312	  harbor_plugin="$(jq -r '.harbor_plugin // ""' <<<"${install_spec}")"
1313	  command_spec="$(printf '%s' "${profile_json}" | "${adapter_path}" command)" \
1314	    || die "harness-adapter ${harness_adapter} command failed"
1315	
1316	  local env_json secret_keys_json command_json env_redacted
1317	  env_json="$(jq -c '.env // {}' <<<"${command_spec}")"
1318	  secret_keys_json="$(jq -c '.secret_env_keys // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
1319	  command_json="$(jq -c '.command // []' <<<"${command_spec}")"
1320	  env_redacted="$(compose_env_redacted "${env_json}" "${secret_keys_json}")"
1321	
1322	  local tasks_dir
1323	  tasks_dir="$(resolve_tasks_dir "${framework}")"
1324	
1325	  local task_spec
1326	  task_spec="$(jq -nc \
1327	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
1328	    --arg tasks_dir "${tasks_dir}" \
1329	    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
1330	    --arg plugin "${harbor_plugin}" \
1331	    --arg image "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" \
1332	    --argjson env "${env_json}" \
1333	    --argjson secret_env_keys "${secret_keys_json}" \
1334	    --arg job_name "${cell_id}" \
1335	    '{task_id:$task_id, tasks_dir:$tasks_dir, cell_dir:$cell_dir,
1336	      harbor_plugin:$plugin, image:$image, env:$env,
1337	      secret_env_keys:$secret_env_keys,
1338	      extra_args:["--jobs-dir","/output","--job-name",$job_name]}')"
1339	
1340	  local executor="${TASK_EXECUTORS_DIR}/harbor"
1341	  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE:-}" ]]; then
1342	    executor="${BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE}"
1343	  fi
1344	  [[ -x "${executor}" ]] || die "task-executor missing/not executable: ${executor}"
1345	
1346	  local exec_status=0
1347	  run_executor_with_retry "${task_spec}" "${cell_dir}" "${executor}" || exec_status=$?
1348	
1349	  local finished_at
1350	  finished_at="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
1351	
1352	  local result_json="{}"
1353	  if [[ -s "${cell_dir}/result.json" ]]; then
1354	    if jq -e '.' "${cell_dir}/result.json" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
1355	      result_json="$(cat "${cell_dir}/result.json")"
1356	    fi
1357	  fi
1358	
1359	  local final_status invalid_class
1360	  if [[ ${exec_status} -eq 0 ]]; then
1361	    final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // .status // "completed"' <<<"${result_json}")"
1362	    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' <<<"${result_json}")"
1363	  else
1364	    final_status="transient_exhausted"
1365	    invalid_class="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // "transient_exhausted"' <<<"${result_json}")"
1366	  fi
1367	
1368	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.txt" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.txt"
1369	  [[ -f "${cell_dir}/fiz.err" ]] || : >"${cell_dir}/fiz.err"
1370	  mkdir -p "${cell_dir}/session"
1371	
1372	  local rep_str
1373	  rep_str="$(printf '%d/%d' "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}")"
1374	
1375	  local cost_usd
1376	  cost_usd="$(compute_cell_cost "${result_json}" "${profile_json}")"
1377	  [[ -n "${cost_usd}" ]] || cost_usd="0"
1378	
1379	  local report
1380	  report="$(jq -n \
1381	    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
1382	    --argjson command "${command_json}" \
1383	    --argjson env_redacted "${env_redacted}" \
1384	    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
1385	    --arg task_id "${task}" \
1386	    --arg framework "${framework}" \
1387	    --arg dataset "${dataset}" \
1388	    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
1389	    --arg started_at "${started_at}" \
1390	    --arg finished_at "${finished_at}" \
1391	    --arg final_status "${final_status}" \
1392	    --arg invalid_class "${invalid_class}" \
1393	    --arg te_ver "${te_ver}" \
1394	    --arg hd "${hd}" \
1395	    --arg rep "${rep_str}" \
1396	    --arg attempt_of "${attempt_of}" \
1397	    --argjson cost "${cost_usd}" \
1398	    '{
1399	       task_id:$task_id,
1400	       framework:$framework,
1401	       dataset:$dataset,
1402	       cell_id:$cell_id,
1403	       started_at:$started_at,
1404	       finished_at:$finished_at,
1405	       rep:$rep,
1406	       profile:$profile,
1407	       command:$command,
1408	       env_redacted:$env_redacted,
1409	       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
1410	       result:$result,
1411	       final_status:$final_status,
1412	       invalid_class:$invalid_class,
1413	       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
1414	       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
1415	       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
1416	     } | if $attempt_of != "" then . + {attempt_of:$attempt_of} else . end')"
1417	
1418	  printf '%s\n' "${report}" >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
1419	  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
1420	
1421	  rm -f "${cell_dir}/cell-state.json"
1422	
1423	  if [[ -n "${attempt_of}" && -f "${attempt_of}/report.json" ]]; then
1424	    local tmp
1425	    tmp="$(mktemp)"
1426	    jq --arg sup "${cell_id}" '. + {superseded_by:$sup}' \
1427	      "${attempt_of}/report.json" >"${tmp}"
1428	    mv "${tmp}" "${attempt_of}/report.json"
1429	  elif [[ -n "${attempt_of}" ]]; then
1430	    # Orphan prior cell with no report.json: write a minimal stub so the
1431	    # superseded_by back-link is recorded.
1432	    jq -n \
1433	      --arg sup "${cell_id}" \
1434	      --arg orphan_of "$(basename "${attempt_of}")" \
1435	      '{final_status:"orphan", invalid_class:"orphan_cell_state", cell_id:$orphan_of, superseded_by:$sup}' \
1436	      >"${attempt_of}/report.json"
1437	    rm -f "${attempt_of}/cell-state.json"
1438	  fi
1439	
1440	  # Unregister from in-flight (trap will also call this on return)
1441	  unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"
1442	  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1443	}
1444	
1445	# run_cell_with_per_cell_retry invokes run_one_cell with per-cell retry logic
1446	# for transient errors. Each retry attempt gets a fresh cell_id and cell_dir,
1447	# linked via attempt_of/superseded_by. Returns the final cell_dir.
1448	run_cell_with_per_cell_retry() {
1449	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1450	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1451	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}"
1452	  local te_ver="${11}" hd="${12}" interrupted_flag="${13:-}"
1453	
1454	  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
1455	  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
1456	  local attempt=1
1457	  local prior_cell_dir=""
1458	
1459	  while :; do
1460	    local cell_dir
1461	    cell_dir="$(run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
1462	                  "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
1463	                  "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${adapter}" "${prior_cell_dir}" \
1464	                  "${te_ver}" "${hd}" "${interrupted_flag}")"
1465	
1466	    if [[ ! -f "${cell_dir}/report.json" ]]; then
1467	      # No result to check, just return the cell
1468	      printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1469	      return 0
1470	    fi
1471	
1472	    local error_class final_status
1473	    error_class="$(jq -r '.error_class // ""' "${cell_dir}/report.json" 2>/dev/null || printf '')"
1474	    final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${cell_dir}/report.json" 2>/dev/null || printf '')"
1475	
1476	    # If non-transient error or success, stop retrying
1477	    if [[ -z "${error_class}" ]] || ! is_transient_error_class "${error_class}"; then
1478	      printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1479	      return 0
1480	    fi
1481	
1482	    # If max attempts reached, mark final cell with transient_exhausted and stop
1483	    if (( attempt >= max_attempts )); then
1484	      local tmp
1485	      tmp="$(mktemp)"
1486	      jq '. + {final_status:"transient_exhausted"}' "${cell_dir}/report.json" >"${tmp}"
1487	      mv "${tmp}" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
1488	      printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1489	      return 0
1490	    fi
1491	
1492	    # Transient error and more attempts available: sleep and retry
1493	    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}/${max_attempts}); sleeping ${backoff}s before retry"
1494	    sleep "${backoff}"
1495	    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
1496	    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
1497	    prior_cell_dir="${cell_dir}"
1498	  done
1499	}
1500	
1501	# budget_or_run_cell either writes a budget-halted placeholder (when the
1502	# budget cap has been hit) or invokes run_cell_with_per_cell_retry. On success
1503	# it appends the cell's cost into <out>/budget.json so the halt flag flips for
1504	# the next cell. cap="" disables budget machinery entirely. interrupted_flag=""
1505	# disables interrupt checking.
1506	budget_or_run_cell() {
1507	  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
1508	  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
1509	  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}"
1510	  local te_ver="${11}" hd="${12}" cap="${13}" interrupted_flag="${14:-}"
1511	
1512	  local cell_dir
1513	  if [[ -n "${cap}" ]] && budget_is_halted "${out}"; then
1514	    cell_dir="$(write_budget_halted_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
1515	                  "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
1516	                  "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${cap}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}")"
1517	    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1518	    return 0
1519	  fi
1520	
1521	  cell_dir="$(run_cell_with_per_cell_retry "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
1522	                "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
1523	                "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${adapter}" \
1524	                "${te_ver}" "${hd}" "${interrupted_flag}")"
1525	
1526	  if [[ -n "${cap}" && -f "${cell_dir}/report.json" ]]; then
1527	    local cell_id cost
1528	    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id // ""' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"
1529	    cost="$(jq -r '.cost_usd_at_run_time // 0' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"
1530	    [[ -n "${cost}" ]] || cost=0
1531	    append_budget_cell "${out}" "${cell_id}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" "${cost}" >/dev/null
1532	  fi
1533	  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
1534	}
1535	
1536	# run_sweep walks the (profile, bench-set, task, rep) matrix, applies resume /
1537	# retry rules, and runs one cell per slot. Supports concurrency-group locking,
1538	# background job pool (--jobs), and signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM).
1539	run_sweep() {
1540	  local profiles_csv="$1" bench_sets_csv="$2" out="$3"
1541	  local force_rerun="$4" retry_invalid="$5" reps_override="$6"
1542	  local max_cost_usd="$7" jobs="${8:-1}"
1543	
1544	  require_tool yq
1545	  require_tool jq
1546	  require_tool sha256sum
1547	  require_tool flock
1548	  require_tool setsid
1549	
1550	  mkdir -p "${out}" "${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}"
1551	
1552	  local te_ver hd
1553	  te_ver="$(task_executor_version)"
1554	  hd="$(harbor_runner_image_digest)"
1555	  write_sweep_metadata "${out}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}"
1556	
1557	  if [[ -n "${max_cost_usd}" ]]; then
1558	    init_budget "${out}" "${max_cost_usd}"
1559	  fi
1560	
1561	  # Signal handling: set up interrupt flag and trap
1562	  local interrupted_flag
1563	  interrupted_flag="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/.benchmark-interrupt-$$"
1564	
1565	  # Trap handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM: interrupt running cells, wait for cleanup
1566	  handle_interrupt() {
1567	    local grace_seconds="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS}"
1568	    [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && return 0
1569	    warn "Received signal, stopping cell submission and interrupting running cells..."
1570	    touch "${interrupted_flag}"
1571	
1572	    # Terminate in-flight cells
1573	    interrupt_inflight_cells
1574	
1575	    # Send SIGTERM to all child processes (the background job pool)
1576	    local pids
1577	    pids="$(jobs -p)"
1578	    if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
1579	      kill -TERM ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
1580	
1581	      # Wait up to grace_seconds for them to exit
1582	      local elapsed=0
1583	      while (( elapsed < grace_seconds )); do
1584	        pids="$(jobs -p)"
1585	        if [[ -z "${pids}" ]]; then
1586	          break
1587	        fi
1588	        sleep 1
1589	        elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
1590	      done
1591	
1592	      # Force kill any stragglers
1593	      pids="$(jobs -p)"
1594	      if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
1595	        kill -KILL ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
1596	      fi
1597	    fi
1598	  }
1599	
1600	  trap 'handle_interrupt' SIGINT SIGTERM
1601	
1602	  local active_jobs=0
1603	
1604	  IFS=',' read -r -a profile_ids <<<"${profiles_csv}"
1605	  IFS=',' read -r -a bench_set_ids <<<"${bench_sets_csv}"
1606	
1607	  local profile_id bs_id bs_file profile_file
1608	  local framework dataset dataset_dir reps tasks harness adapter
1609	  local terminal_file invalid_file orphan_file
1610	  local concurrency_group
1611	
1612	  for profile_id in "${profile_ids[@]}"; do
1613	    [[ -n "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
1614	    profile_file="$(resolve_profile_file "${profile_id}")"
1615	    local profile_json_full harness
1616	    profile_json_full="$(yq -o=json '.' "${profile_file}")"
1617	    harness="$(jq -r '.harness // "none"' <<<"${profile_json_full}")"
1618	    adapter="$(resolve_adapter_for_harness "${harness}")"
1619	    concurrency_group="$(jq -r '.concurrency_group // "default"' <<<"${profile_json_full}")"
1620	
1621	    for bs_id in "${bench_set_ids[@]}"; do
1622	      [[ -n "${bs_id}" ]] || continue
1623	      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
1624	      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
1625	      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
1626	      dataset_dir="${dataset}"
1627	      if [[ -n "${reps_override}" ]]; then
1628	        reps="${reps_override}"
1629	      else
1630	        reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
1631	      fi
1632	      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
1633	
1634	      local task
1635	      while IFS= read -r task; do
1636	        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
1637	        [[ "${task}" != "*all*" ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: tasks: all is not supported by run path yet"
1638	
1639	        terminal_file="$(mktemp)"; invalid_file="$(mktemp)"; orphan_file="$(mktemp)"
1640	        inspect_existing_cells "${out}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" \
1641	          "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
1642	
1643	        local terminal_count invalid_count orphan_count
1644	        terminal_count="$(wc -l <"${terminal_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
1645	        invalid_count="$(wc -l <"${invalid_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
1646	        orphan_count="$(wc -l <"${orphan_file}" | tr -d ' ')"
1647	
1648	        local cells_to_run=()
1649	        if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 ]]; then
1650	          local r
1651	          for ((r = 1; r <= reps; r++)); do
1652	            cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|${r}|${reps}|${adapter}|${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
1653	          done
1654	        elif [[ ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
1655	          local prior
1656	          while IFS= read -r prior; do
1657	            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
1658	            cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|1|${reps}|${adapter}|${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
1659	          done <"${invalid_file}"
1660	          while IFS= read -r prior; do
1661	            [[ -n "${prior}" ]] || continue
1662	            cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|1|${reps}|${adapter}|${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
1663	          done <"${orphan_file}"
1664	          warn "retry-invalid: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} reran invalid=${invalid_count} orphan=${orphan_count}"
1665	        else
1666	          if [[ ${terminal_count} -ge ${reps} ]]; then
1667	            warn "skip: profile=${profile_id} task=${task} (terminal_cells=${terminal_count} >= reps=${reps})"
1668	          else
1669	            local need r idx
1670	            need=$((reps - terminal_count))
1671	            idx=$((terminal_count + 1))
1672	            for ((r = 1; r <= need; r++)); do
1673	              cells_to_run+=("${profile_id}|${profile_file}|${framework}|${dataset}|${dataset_dir}|${task}|${idx}|${reps}|${adapter}|${te_ver}|${hd}|${max_cost_usd}|${concurrency_group}")
1674	              idx=$((idx + 1))
1675	            done
1676	          fi
1677	        fi
1678	
1679	        # Run cells with job pool and concurrency-group locking
1680	        for cell_spec in "${cells_to_run[@]}"; do
1681	          # Wait for a job slot to become available
1682	          while (( active_jobs >= jobs )); do
1683	            wait -n 2>/dev/null || true
1684	            active_jobs=$((active_jobs - 1))
1685	          done
1686	
1687	          # Check if we've been interrupted
1688	          if [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]]; then
1689	            warn "Stopping cell submission due to signal interrupt"
1690	            # Wait for remaining jobs
1691	            wait
1692	            rm -f "${interrupted_flag}" "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
1693	            trap - SIGINT SIGTERM
1694	            return 130
1695	          fi
1696	
1697	          # Parse cell spec
1698	          IFS='|' read -r p_id p_file fw ds ds_dir tsk rep_idx t_reps adp te_v hd_v cap cg <<<"${cell_spec}"
1699	
1700	          # Acquire concurrency-group lock
1701	          local lock_fd
1702	          lock_fd="$(acquire_group_lock "${cg}")"
1703	
1704	          # Run cell in a fresh session so the worker PID is a killable
1705	          # process-group leader for signal handling.
1706	          export_cell_worker_functions
1707	          launch_cell_worker "${lock_fd}" \
1708	            "${out}" "${p_id}" "${p_file}" \
1709	            "${fw}" "${ds}" "${ds_dir}" "${tsk}" \
1710	            "${rep_idx}" "${t_reps}" "${adp}" \
1711	            "${te_v}" "${hd_v}" "${cap}" "${interrupted_flag}"
1712	
1713	          active_jobs=$((active_jobs + 1))
1714	        done
1715	
1716	        rm -f "${terminal_file}" "${invalid_file}" "${orphan_file}"
1717	      done <<<"${tasks}"
1718	    done
1719	  done
1720	
1721	  # Wait for all background jobs to complete
1722	  wait
1723	  local exit_code=0
1724	  [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && exit_code=130
1725	  rm -f "${interrupted_flag}"
1726	  trap - SIGINT SIGTERM
1727	  return "${exit_code}"
1728	}
1729	
1730	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1731	# validate checks the offline manifest surface without touching the network.
1732	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1733	
1734	cmd_validate() {
1735	  require_tool yq
1736	  require_tool jq
1737	
1738	  local tmpdir allowlist_file concurrency_json synthetic_profile synthetic_task_spec
1739	  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
1740	  allowlist_file="${tmpdir}/task-allowlist.txt"
1741	
1742	  build_task_allowlist_file "${allowlist_file}"
1743	  concurrency_json="$(concurrency_groups_json)"
1744	  jq -e '
1745	    (.groups // {}) | type == "object"
1746	    and (to_entries | length > 0)
1747	    and all(to_entries[]; (.value.max_concurrency | type == "number") and (.value.max_concurrency > 0))
1748	  ' <<<"${concurrency_json}" >/dev/null \
1749	    || die "validate: concurrency-groups.yaml is malformed"
1750	
1751	  validate_profile_catalog "${concurrency_json}"
1752	  validate_bench_sets "${allowlist_file}"
1753	
1754	  synthetic_profile="$(jq -nc '{
1755	    id: "validate-profile",
1756	    harness: "none",
1757	    surface: "fiz_provider_native",
1758	    concurrency_group: "openrouter",
1759	    provider: {
1760	      type: "openrouter",
1761	      model: "openai/gpt-5.4-mini",
1762	      base_url: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
1763	      api_key_env: "OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
1764	    },
1765	    sampling: {
1766	      temperature: 0.0,
1767	      reasoning: "low",
1768	      planning_mode: true,
1769	      top_p: 0.95,
1770	      top_k: 20,
1771	      min_p: 0
1772	    },
1773	    limits: {
1774	      max_output_tokens: 128000,
1775	      context_tokens: 1000000
1776	    },
1777	    metadata: {
1778	      runtime: "validate"
1779	    }
1780	  }')"
1781	  validate_harness_adapters "${tmpdir}" "${synthetic_profile}"
1782	
1783	  mkdir -p "${tmpdir}/tasks" "${tmpdir}/cell"
1784	  synthetic_task_spec="$(jq -nc --arg tasks_dir "${tmpdir}/tasks" --arg cell_dir "${tmpdir}/cell" '{
1785	    task_id: "validate-task",
1786	    tasks_dir: $tasks_dir,
1787	    cell_dir: $cell_dir,
1788	    harbor_plugin: "scripts.benchmark.harbor_agent:FizeauAgent",
1789	    image: "fizeau-harbor-runner:latest",
1790	    env: {
1791	      FIZEAU_BASE_URL: "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
1792	      FIZEAU_MODEL: "openai/gpt-5.4-mini",
1793	      FIZEAU_API_KEY: "${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}"
1794	    },

1800	  return 0
1801	}

1803	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1804	# main dispatch
1805	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1806	
1807	main() {
1808	  if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
1809	    usage
1810	    exit 2
1811	  fi
1812	
1813	  local profile_csv=""
1814	  local bench_set_csv=""
1815	  local plan=0
1816	  local out=""
1817	  local force_rerun=0
1818	  local retry_invalid=0
1819	  local reps_override=""
1820	  local max_cost_usd=""
1821	  local jobs=1
1822	  local positional=()
1823	
1824	  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
1825	    case "$1" in
1826	      --profile)        profile_csv="${2:?--profile requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
1827	      --bench-set)      bench_set_csv="${2:?--bench-set requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
1828	      --plan)           plan=1; shift ;;
1829	      --out)            out="${2:?--out requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
1830	      --force-rerun)    force_rerun=1; shift ;;
1831	      --retry-invalid)  retry_invalid=1; shift ;;
1832	      --reps)           reps_override="${2:?--reps requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
1833	      --max-cost-usd)   max_cost_usd="${2:?--max-cost-usd requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
1834	      --jobs)           jobs="${2:?--jobs requires a value}"; shift 2 ;;
1835	      -h|--help|help)   usage; exit 0 ;;
1836	      --)               shift; positional+=("$@"); break ;;
1837	      -*)               die "unknown flag: $1" ;;
1838	      *)                positional+=("$1"); shift ;;
1839	    esac
1840	  done
1841	
1842	  local sub=""
1843	  if [[ ${#positional[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
1844	    sub="${positional[0]}"
1845	  fi
1846	
1847	  case "${sub}" in
1848	    profiles)          cmd_profiles ;;
1849	    bench-sets)        cmd_bench_sets ;;
1850	    harness-adapters)  cmd_harness_adapters ;;
1851	    task-executors)    cmd_task_executors ;;
1852	    validate)          cmd_validate ;;
1853	    preflight)         cmd_preflight ;;
1854	    "")
1855	      require_tool yq
1856	      require_tool jq
1857	      [[ -n "${profile_csv}" ]]   || die "--profile is required"
1858	      [[ -n "${bench_set_csv}" ]] || die "--bench-set is required"
1859	      if [[ ${plan} -eq 1 ]]; then
1860	        print_plan "${profile_csv}" "${bench_set_csv}" "${reps_override}"
1861	        exit 0
1862	      fi
1863	      if [[ -z "${out}" ]]; then
1864	        out="${DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT}"
1865	      fi
1866	      if [[ ${force_rerun} -eq 1 && ${retry_invalid} -eq 1 ]]; then
1867	        die "--force-rerun and --retry-invalid are mutually exclusive"
1868	      fi
1869	      if [[ -n "${max_cost_usd}" ]]; then
1870	        # Validate numeric and non-negative; jq parses with no locale surprises.
1871	        if ! printf '%s' "${max_cost_usd}" \
1872	              | jq -e 'tonumber | . >= 0' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
1873	          die "--max-cost-usd requires a non-negative number (got: ${max_cost_usd})"
1874	        fi
1875	      fi
1876	      run_sweep "${profile_csv}" "${bench_set_csv}" "${out}" \
1877	        "${force_rerun}" "${retry_invalid}" "${reps_override}" \
1878	        "${max_cost_usd}" "${jobs}"
1879	      ;;
1880	    *)
1881	      die "unknown subcommand: ${sub}"
1882	      ;;
1883	  esac
1884	}
1885	
1886	main "$@"

# interrupt_inflight_cells is called during signal handling to mark in-flight
# cells as interrupted and clean them up from the JSON file.
interrupt_inflight_cells() {
  local json_path grace_seconds="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS:-30}"
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0

  local cells
  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}")" || return 0

  local cell_id cell_dir pid
  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"

    # Try to kill the cell's process group
    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
      kill -TERM -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || kill -TERM "${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi

    # Try to docker stop any harbor-runner containers in this cell
    if [[ -d "${cell_dir}" ]]; then
      docker ps -q --filter "label=fizeau.cell-id=${cell_id}" 2>/dev/null \
        | xargs -r docker stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
  done <<<"${cells}"

  # Wait for grace period before force-killing stragglers
  local elapsed=0
  while (( elapsed < grace_seconds )); do
    local remaining
    remaining="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)" || remaining=0
    if (( remaining == 0 )); then
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
    elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
  done

  # Force kill any remaining process groups
  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}" 2>/dev/null)" || return 0
  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
      kill -KILL -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || kill -KILL "${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
  done <<<"${cells}"
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_runner_signals.sh — acceptance tests for signal handling (SIGTERM/SIGINT)
# Tests A3b: SIGTERM/SIGINT trapping, docker container cleanup, interrupted reports.
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
BENCHMARK_BIN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

fail() {
  echo -e "${RED}FAIL${NC}: $*" >&2
  TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
}

pass() {
  echo -e "${GREEN}PASS${NC}: $*"
  TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
}

# test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers: AC1
# Spawn benchmark, wait 5s, SIGTERM it, verify exit code != 0,
# in-flight cells have final_status=="interrupted" and process_outcome=="killed",
# no docker containers remain with fizeau.cell-id label.
test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers() {
  local test_name="test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers"
  local tmpdir results_dir runner_pid runner_exit_code
  local in_flight_json interrupted_count

  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  results_dir="${tmpdir}/bench/results"
  trap "rm -rf '${tmpdir}'; jobs -p | xargs -r kill -9 2>/dev/null || true" RETURN

  mkdir -p "${results_dir}"

  # Spawn benchmark in background
  cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}" && \
  DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT="${results_dir}" \
  FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${tmpdir}/.cache/fizeau-benchmark" \
  ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary --jobs 1 &
  runner_pid=$!

  # Wait for runner to start and register cells
  sleep 5

  # Send SIGTERM to the runner
  kill -TERM "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Wait for runner to exit
  set +e
  wait "${runner_pid}"
  runner_exit_code=$?
  set -e

  # Verify runner exited with non-zero
  if [[ ${runner_exit_code} -eq 0 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: expected non-zero exit code on SIGTERM, got ${runner_exit_code}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Verify no docker containers remain with fizeau.cell-id label
  local remaining_containers
  remaining_containers="$(docker ps -q --filter 'label=fizeau.cell-id' 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
  if [[ -n "${remaining_containers}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: docker containers remain after SIGTERM: $(echo "${remaining_containers}" | tr '\n' ' ')"
    docker ps --filter 'label=fizeau.cell-id' >&2 || true
    return 1
  fi

  # Check for interrupted cells in the results directory
  interrupted_count=0
  if [[ -d "${results_dir}/cells" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r report; do
      if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
        local final_status process_outcome
        final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
        process_outcome="$(jq -r '.process_outcome // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
        if [[ "${final_status}" == "interrupted" && "${process_outcome}" == "killed" ]]; then
          interrupted_count=$((interrupted_count + 1))
        fi
      fi
    done < <(find "${results_dir}/cells" -name 'report.json' -type f)
  fi

  if [[ ${interrupted_count} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: ${test_name}: no interrupted cells found in results (may be expected if runner was killed before any cells started)" >&2
  fi

  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_sigint_same_behavior: AC2
# Same as test_sigterm but with SIGINT.
test_sigint_same_behavior() {
  local test_name="test_sigint_same_behavior"
  local tmpdir results_dir runner_pid runner_exit_code
  local in_flight_json interrupted_count

  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  results_dir="${tmpdir}/bench/results"
  trap "rm -rf '${tmpdir}'; jobs -p | xargs -r kill -9 2>/dev/null || true" RETURN

  mkdir -p "${results_dir}"

  # Spawn benchmark in background
  cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}" && \
  DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT="${results_dir}" \
  FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${tmpdir}/.cache/fizeau-benchmark" \
  ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary --jobs 1 &
  runner_pid=$!

  # Wait for runner to start and register cells
  sleep 5

  # Send SIGINT to the runner
  kill -INT "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Wait for runner to exit
  set +e
  wait "${runner_pid}"
  runner_exit_code=$?
  set -e

  # Verify runner exited with non-zero
  if [[ ${runner_exit_code} -eq 0 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: expected non-zero exit code on SIGINT, got ${runner_exit_code}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Verify no docker containers remain with fizeau.cell-id label
  local remaining_containers
  remaining_containers="$(docker ps -q --filter 'label=fizeau.cell-id' 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
  if [[ -n "${remaining_containers}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: docker containers remain after SIGINT: $(echo "${remaining_containers}" | tr '\n' ' ')"
    docker ps --filter 'label=fizeau.cell-id' >&2 || true
    return 1
  fi

  # Check for interrupted cells in the results directory
  interrupted_count=0
  if [[ -d "${results_dir}/cells" ]]; then
    while IFS= read -r report; do
      if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
        local final_status process_outcome
        final_status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
        process_outcome="$(jq -r '.process_outcome // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
        if [[ "${final_status}" == "interrupted" && "${process_outcome}" == "killed" ]]; then
          interrupted_count=$((interrupted_count + 1))
        fi
      fi
    done < <(find "${results_dir}/cells" -name 'report.json' -type f)
  fi

  if [[ ${interrupted_count} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: ${test_name}: no interrupted cells found in results (may be expected if runner was killed before any cells started)" >&2
  fi

  pass "${test_name}"
}

# test_sigkill_after_grace_period: AC3
# A child process that ignores SIGTERM is force-killed after grace period.
# Verify the report is still written with interrupted status.
test_sigkill_after_grace_period() {
  local test_name="test_sigkill_after_grace_period"
  local tmpdir results_dir runner_pid runner_exit_code
  local grace_seconds=5

  tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
  results_dir="${tmpdir}/bench/results"
  trap "rm -rf '${tmpdir}'; jobs -p | xargs -r kill -9 2>/dev/null || true" RETURN

  mkdir -p "${results_dir}"

  # Spawn benchmark with short grace period for testing
  cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}" && \
  DEFAULT_OUT_ROOT="${results_dir}" \
  FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR="${tmpdir}/.cache/fizeau-benchmark" \
  BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS="${grace_seconds}" \
  ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary --jobs 1 &
  runner_pid=$!

  # Wait for runner to start
  sleep 3

  # Send SIGTERM
  kill -TERM "${runner_pid}" 2>/dev/null || true

  # Wait for runner to exit (should take ~grace_seconds to force-kill stragglers)
  set +e
  wait "${runner_pid}"
  runner_exit_code=$?
  set -e

  # Verify runner exited with non-zero
  if [[ ${runner_exit_code} -eq 0 ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: expected non-zero exit code, got ${runner_exit_code}"
    return 1
  fi

  # Verify no docker containers remain
  local remaining_containers
  remaining_containers="$(docker ps -q --filter 'label=fizeau.cell-id' 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
  if [[ -n "${remaining_containers}" ]]; then
    fail "${test_name}: docker containers remain after force-kill: $(echo "${remaining_containers}" | tr '\n' ' ')"
    docker ps --filter 'label=fizeau.cell-id' >&2 || true
    return 1
  fi

  pass "${test_name}"
}

# main
main() {
  cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}"

  # Ensure Docker is available
  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "ERROR: docker not available, skipping signal tests" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  echo "Running signal handling tests..."
  test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers || true
  test_sigint_same_behavior || true
  test_sigkill_after_grace_period || true

  echo ""
  echo "Tests passed: ${TESTS_PASSED}"
  echo "Tests failed: ${TESTS_FAILED}"

  if [[ ${TESTS_FAILED} -gt 0 ]]; then
    return 1
  fi
}

main "$@"

data.text: I see the issue - the signal handler needs to actually cause an early exit. Let me update the `handle_interrupt` function to call `exit` directly:

input.old_string:
# Trap handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM: interrupt running cells, wait for cleanup
  handle_interrupt() {
    local grace_seconds="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS}"
    [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && return 0
    warn "Received signal, stopping cell submission and interrupting running cells..."
    touch "${interrupted_flag}"

    # Terminate in-flight cells
    interrupt_inflight_cells

    # Send SIGTERM to all child processes (the background job pool)
    local pids
    pids="$(jobs -p)"
    if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
      kill -TERM ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true

      # Wait up to grace_seconds for them to exit
      local elapsed=0
      while (( elapsed < grace_seconds )); do
        pids="$(jobs -p)"
        if [[ -z "${pids}" ]]; then
          break
        fi
        sleep 1
        elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
      done

      # Force kill any stragglers
      pids="$(jobs -p)"
      if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
        kill -KILL ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    fi
  }

  trap 'handle_interrupt' SIGINT SIGTERM
input.new_string:
# Trap handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM: interrupt running cells, wait for cleanup
  handle_interrupt() {
    local grace_seconds="${BENCH_TERM_GRACE_SECONDS}"
    [[ -f "${interrupted_flag}" ]] && return 0
    warn "Received signal, stopping cell submission and interrupting running cells..."
    touch "${interrupted_flag}"

    # Terminate in-flight cells
    interrupt_inflight_cells

    # Send SIGTERM to all child processes (the background job pool)
    local pids
    pids="$(jobs -p)"
    if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
      kill -TERM ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true

      # Wait up to grace_seconds for them to exit
      local elapsed=0
      while (( elapsed < grace_seconds )); do
        pids="$(jobs -p)"
        if [[ -z "${pids}" ]]; then
          break
        fi
        sleep 1
        elapsed=$((elapsed + 1))
      done

      # Force kill any stragglers
      pids="$(jobs -p)"
      if [[ -n "${pids}" ]]; then
        kill -KILL ${pids} 2>/dev/null || true
      fi
    fi

    # Exit immediately on signal
    exit 130
  }

  trap 'handle_interrupt' SIGINT SIGTERM

Implement signal handling for the benchmark runner (A3b):

   - final_status="interrupted"
   - process_outcome="killed"
   - All required fields (profile, command, artifacts, etc.)

   - test_sigterm_marks_interrupted_and_stops_containers
   - test_sigint_same_behavior
   - test_sigkill_after_grace_period

All tests verify:
- Runner exits with code 130 on signal
- Docker containers are cleaned up (no containers with fizeau.cell-id label remain)
- In-flight cells receive interrupted status (when applicable)

Satisfies A3b acceptance criteria and coordinates with A3a (in-flight.json usage).
